Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Microscopes can see video on a laserdisc 320 upvotes, 26 comments
  2. Artificial-life: A simple (300 lines of code) reproduction of Computational Life 83 upvotes, 9 comments
  3. Z80 Sans – a disassembler in a font (2024) 89 upvotes, 10 comments
  4. FrameBook 385 upvotes, 65 comments
  5. LibreOffice Writer now supports Markdown 308 upvotes, 55 comments
  6. Agent Safehouse – macOS-native sandboxing for local agents 434 upvotes, 96 comments
  7. PCB devboard the size of a USB-C plug 100 upvotes, 19 comments
  8. Pushing and Pulling: Three reactivity algorithms 84 upvotes, 16 comments
  9. What if the Apple ][ had run on Field-Sequential? 85 upvotes, 17 comments
  10. Every single board computer I tested in 2025 131 upvotes, 34 comments
  11. Linux Internals: How /proc/self/mem writes to unwritable memory (2021) 48 upvotes, 10 comments
  12. Lil Finder Guy 91 upvotes, 26 comments
  13. Show HN: I built a real-time OSINT dashboard pulling 15 live global feeds 196 upvotes, 87 comments
  14. I made a programming language with M&Ms 57 upvotes, 23 comments
  15. We should revisit literate programming in the agent era 182 upvotes, 97 comments
  16. Beagle, a source code management system that stores AST trees 86 upvotes, 44 comments
  17. WSL Manager 82 upvotes, 47 comments
  18. The Death of Social Media Is the Renaissance of RSS 14 upvotes, 4 comments
  19. Blacksky AppView 118 upvotes, 73 comments
  20. Ask HN: Please restrict new accounts from posting 514 upvotes, 391 comments
  21. Ask HN: How to be alone? 341 upvotes, 253 comments
  22. The legendary Mojave Phone Booth is back (2013) 15 upvotes, 5 comments
  23. Why can't you tune your guitar? (2019) 189 upvotes, 134 comments
  24. Show HN: Skir – like Protocol Buffer but better 68 upvotes, 42 comments
  25. My Homelab Setup 180 upvotes, 131 comments
  26. Show HN: Reviving a 20-year-old puzzle game Chromatron with Ghidra and AI 6 upvotes, 1 comments
  27. My “grand vision” for Rust 146 upvotes, 122 comments
  28. Living human brain cells play DOOM on a CL1 [video] 160 upvotes, 153 comments
  29. Last Statements 17 upvotes, 12 comments
  30. Ask HN: What Are You Working On? (March 2026) 78 upvotes, 254 comments

Last generated 1:00pm, 9 March 2026. Source here.
